title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to modify the railroad track maintenance credit.
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to a tax credit that helps railroad companies pay for maintaining their train tracks. It increases the tax credit from $3,500 to $6,100 for each mile of track that companies own or lease. Starting in 2026, the credit amount will go up each year to keep up with inflation. The bill also lets companies use the credit for tracks they got as recently as 2024, instead of only tracks they had before 2015. These changes will help railroad companies, especially smaller ones, afford to keep their tracks in good condition.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Modification of Railroad Track Maintenance Credit
sections.text: This section changes the tax credit that companies can claim for maintaining railroad tracks. The credit amount increases from three thousand five hundred dollars to six thousand one hundred dollars per mile of track. Starting in two thousand twenty six, this credit amount will go up each year based on inflation using the same method the government uses to adjust other tax amounts. Any inflation increase will be rounded to the nearest one hundred dollars. The section also updates the rules so that track maintenance work done starting January first two thousand twenty four qualifies for this credit, instead of the previous date of January first two thousand fifteen. These changes apply to money spent on track maintenance in tax years that begin after December thirty first two thousand twenty four.
